steps.title: Set up the product
steps.content: With $g(x)=\frac{2}{7}x+\frac{8}{5}$ and $h(x)=5x-7$, the product is $$g(x)\cdot h(x) = \left(\frac{2}{7}x+\frac{8}{5}\right)(5x-7).$$ Use FOIL to expand.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: The two linear terms add: $-2x + 8x = 6x$. The full expression becomes $$\frac{10x^2}{7} + 6x - \frac{56}{5}.$$

steps.title: Evaluate the choices
steps.content:
• A. $\frac{10x^2}{7}-\frac{56}{5}$ — omits the linear term entirely.
  • B. $\frac{10x^2}{7}+\frac{13x}{6}-\frac{56}{5}$ — wrong linear coefficient.
  • C. $\frac{10x^2}{7}-6x-\frac{56}{5}$ — correct magnitude but wrong sign.
  • D. $\frac{10x^2}{7}+6x-\frac{56}{5}$ — matches the expansion.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
